message:
fix(install): Prevent double-remove of lock file in cleanup trap

Problem
-------
The cleanup_on_exit function could be called multiple times (e.g., from
both an explicit trap handler and the EXIT trap), potentially attempting
to remove the lock directory twice. While rmdir is idempotent (fails
silently on non-existent dirs), it's clearer to explicitly prevent
double-removal.

Solution
--------
Set LOCK_OWNED=0 after successful rmdir to mark that we no longer own
the lock. Subsequent cleanup calls will skip the rmdir attempt.

Impact
------
- Clearer lock ownership semantics
- Prevents spurious errors in edge cases
- No functional change (rmdir already handles missing dirs gracefully)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -63,6 +63,7 @@ cleanup_on_exit() {
   # Remove lock file ONLY if we created it (don't remove another process's lock!)
   if [ "$LOCK_OWNED" -eq 1 ]; then
     rmdir "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null
+    LOCK_OWNED=0  # Mark that we no longer own it (prevents double-remove in EXIT trap)
   fi
 }
